$comment: Generated from types/errors.ts — do not edit
title: AHP Error Codes
description: Error codes used in the Agent Host Protocol.

AuthRequiredErrorData.description:
Details carried in the `data` field of an `AuthRequired` (-32007) error.

Wraps the protected resource list in `{ resources: [...] }` rather than
returning a bare array, so additional fields can be added in future
versions without breaking the wire shape.

PermissionDeniedErrorData.description:
Details carried in the `data` field of a `PermissionDenied` (-32009) error.

The receiver MAY advertise a `resourceRequest` payload describing the
access that, if granted, would unlock the operation. The caller MAY then
issue `resourceRequest` with that payload to negotiate access.

request.description:
The resource access that, if granted via `resourceRequest`, would unlock
the operation. Omitted when no specific access grant would resolve the
denial (for example, when the resource is fundamentally inaccessible).

UnsupportedProtocolVersionErrorData.description:
Details carried in the `data` field of an `UnsupportedProtocolVersion`
(-32005) error.

supportedVersions.description:
Protocol versions the server is willing to speak.

Each entry is either a [SemVer](https://semver.org) `MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH`
string (e.g. `"0.1.0"`) or a [SemVer range](https://semver.org/#spec-item-11)
constraint (e.g. `">=0.1.0 <0.3.0"` or `"^0.2.0"`).

AhpErrorDetailsMap.description:
Maps each AHP error code that carries structured `data` to the type of
that data.

Error codes not present in this map either have no `data` payload or
carry an unspecified payload that callers SHOULD treat as `unknown`.

Icon.description: An optionally-sized icon that can be displayed in a user interface.

src.description:
A standard URI pointing to an icon resource. May be an HTTP/HTTPS URL or a
`data:` URI with Base64-encoded image data.

Consumers SHOULD take steps to ensure URLs serving icons are from the
same domain as the client/server or a trusted domain.

Consumers SHOULD take appropriate precautions when consuming SVGs as they can contain
executable JavaScript.

contentType.description:
Optional MIME type override if the source MIME type is missing or generic.
For example: `"image/png"`, `"image/jpeg"`, or `"image/svg+xml"`.

sizes.description:
Optional array of strings that specify sizes at which the icon can be used.
Each string should be in WxH format (e.g., `"48x48"`, `"96x96"`) or `"any"` for scalable formats like SVG.

If not provided, the client should assume that the icon can be used at any size.

theme.description:
Optional specifier for the theme this icon is designed for. `"light"` indicates
the icon is designed to be used with a light background, and `"dark"` indicates
the icon is designed to be used with a dark background.

If not provided, the client should assume the icon can be used with any theme.

ProtectedResourceMetadata.description:
Describes a protected resource's authentication requirements using
[RFC 9728](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9728) (OAuth 2.0
Protected Resource Metadata) semantics.

Field names use snake_case to match the RFC 9728 JSON format.

resource.description:
REQUIRED. The protected resource's resource identifier, a URL using the
`https` scheme with no fragment component (e.g. `"https://api.github.com"`).

resource_name.description: OPTIONAL. Human-readable name of the protected resource.

authorization_servers.description: OPTIONAL. JSON array of OAuth authorization server identifier URLs.

jwks_uri.description: OPTIONAL. URL of the protected resource's JWK Set document.

scopes_supported.description: RECOMMENDED. JSON array of OAuth 2.0 scope values used in authorization requests.

bearer_methods_supported.description: OPTIONAL. JSON array of Bearer Token presentation methods supported.

resource_signing_alg_values_supported.description: OPTIONAL. JSON array of JWS signing algorithms supported.

resource_encryption_alg_values_supported.description: OPTIONAL. JSON array of JWE encryption algorithms (alg) supported.

resource_encryption_enc_values_supported.description: OPTIONAL. JSON array of JWE encryption algorithms (enc) supported.

resource_documentation.description: OPTIONAL. URL of human-readable documentation for the resource.

resource_policy_uri.description: OPTIONAL. URL of the resource's data-usage policy.

resource_tos_uri.description: OPTIONAL. URL of the resource's terms of service.

required.description:
AHP extension. Whether authentication is required for this resource.

- `true` (default) — the agent cannot be used without a valid token.
  The server SHOULD return `AuthRequired` (`-32007`) if the client
  attempts to use the agent without authenticating.
- `false` — the agent works without authentication but MAY offer
  enhanced capabilities when a token is provided.

Clients SHOULD treat an absent field the same as `true`.

RootState.description: Global state shared with every client subscribed to `agenthost:/root`.

agents.description: Available agent backends and their models

activeSessions.description: Number of active (non-disposed) sessions on the server

terminals.description: Known terminals on the server. Subscribe to individual terminal URIs for full state.

config.description: Agent host configuration schema and current values

protectedResources.description:
Protected resources this agent requires authentication for.

Each entry describes an OAuth 2.0 protected resource using
[RFC 9728](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9728) semantics.
Clients should obtain tokens from the declared `authorization_servers`
and push them via the `authenticate` command before creating sessions
with this agent.

customizations.description:
Customizations (Open Plugins) associated with this agent.

Each entry is a reference to an [Open Plugins](https://open-plugins.com/)
plugin that the agent host can activate for sessions using this agent.

configSchema.description:
Configuration schema describing model-specific options (e.g. thinking
level). Clients present this as a form and pass the resolved values in
{@link ModelSelection.config} when creating or changing sessions.

_meta.description:
Additional provider-specific metadata for this model.

Clients MAY look for well-known keys here to provide enhanced UI.
For example, a `pricing` key may carry model pricing metadata.

ModelSelection.description:
A model selection: the chosen model ID together with any model-specific
configuration values whose keys correspond to the model's
{@link SessionModelInfo.configSchema}.

AgentSelection.description:
An agent selection: the URI of the chosen agent's source `.agent.md`
file (matching `INamedPluginResource.uri` / `ICustomAgent.uri` in the
customization pipeline). URIs are unique by construction, so selection is
unambiguous even when two plugins contribute agents with the same `name`.
Selection is opaque to the protocol — providers translate the URI to their
backend's representation (e.g. the Copilot SDK addresses agents by `name`,
so the Copilot provider does a URI → name lookup at its SDK boundary).

id.description: URI of the agent's source file (a stringified URI; same shape as `ModelSelection.id`).

name.description: Human-readable agent name (e.g. the `name` field declared in the `.agent.md` file).

PendingMessage.description:
A message queued for future delivery to the agent.

Steering messages are injected into the current turn mid-flight.
Queued messages are automatically started as new turns after the
current turn naturally finishes.

id.description: Unique identifier for this pending message

SessionState.description: Full state for a single session, loaded when a client subscribes to the session's URI.

serverTools.description: Tools provided by the server (agent host) for this session

activeClient.description: The client currently providing tools and interactive capabilities to this session

steeringMessage.description: Message to inject into the current turn at a convenient point

queuedMessages.description: Messages to send automatically as new turns after the current turn finishes

inputRequests.description: Requests for user input that are currently blocking or informing session progress

config.description: Session configuration schema and current values

customizations.description:
Server-provided customizations active in this session.

Client-provided customizations are available on
{@link SessionActiveClient.customizations | activeClient.customizations}.

_meta.description:
Additional provider-specific metadata for this session.

Clients MAY look for well-known keys here to provide enhanced UI.
For example, a `git` key may provide extra git metadata about the session's
workingDirectory.

SessionActiveClient.description:
The client currently providing tools and interactive capabilities to a session.

Only one client may be active per session at a time. The server SHOULD
automatically unset the active client if that client disconnects.

tools.description: Tools this client provides to the session

customizations.description: Customizations this client contributes to the session

ProjectInfo.description: Server-owned project metadata for a session.

activity.description: Human-readable description of what the session is currently doing

workingDirectory.description: The working directory URI for this session

diffs.description: Files changed during this session with diff statistics

ConfigPropertySchema.description:
A JSON Schema-compatible property descriptor with display extensions.

Standard JSON Schema fields (`type`, `title`, `description`, `default`,
`enum`) allow validators to process the schema. Display extensions
(`enumLabels`, `enumDescriptions`) are parallel arrays that provide UI
metadata for each `enum` value.

This is the generic base type. See {@link SessionConfigPropertySchema} for
session-specific extensions.

title.description: JSON Schema: human-readable label for the property

enum.description: JSON Schema: allowed values (typically used with `string` type)

enumLabels.description: Display extension: human-readable label per enum value (parallel array)

enumDescriptions.description: Display extension: description per enum value (parallel array)

readOnly.description: JSON Schema: when `true`, the property is displayed but cannot be modified by the user

items.description: JSON Schema: schema for array items (used when `type` is `'array'`)

properties.description: JSON Schema: property descriptors for object properties (used when `type` is `'object'`)

required.description: JSON Schema: list of required property ids (used when `type` is `'object'`)

ConfigSchema.description:
A JSON Schema object describing available configuration properties.

This is the generic base type. See {@link SessionConfigSchema} for
session-specific usage.

properties.description: JSON Schema: property descriptors keyed by property id

required.description: JSON Schema: list of required property ids

RootConfigState.description:
Live agent-host configuration metadata.

The schema describes the available configuration properties and the values
contain the current value for each resolved property.

schema.description: JSON Schema describing available configuration properties

SessionConfigPropertySchema.description:
A session configuration property descriptor.

Extends the generic {@link ConfigPropertySchema} with session-specific
display extensions.

title.description: JSON Schema: human-readable label for the property

enum.description: JSON Schema: allowed values (typically used with `string` type)

enumLabels.description: Display extension: human-readable label per enum value (parallel array)

enumDescriptions.description: Display extension: description per enum value (parallel array)

readOnly.description: JSON Schema: when `true`, the property is displayed but cannot be modified by the user

items.description: JSON Schema: schema for array items (used when `type` is `'array'`)

properties.description: JSON Schema: property descriptors for object properties (used when `type` is `'object'`)

required.description: JSON Schema: list of required property ids (used when `type` is `'object'`)

enumDynamic.description:
Display extension: when `true`, the full set of allowed values is too large
to enumerate statically. The client SHOULD use `sessionConfigCompletions`
to fetch matching values based on user input. Any values in `enum` are
seed/recent values for initial display.

sessionMutable.description: When `true`, the user may change this property after session creation

SessionConfigSchema.description: A JSON Schema object describing available session configuration metadata.

properties.description: JSON Schema: property descriptors keyed by property id

required.description: JSON Schema: list of required property ids

SessionConfigState.description:
Live session configuration metadata.

The schema describes the available configuration properties and the values
contain the current value for each resolved property.

schema.description: JSON Schema describing available configuration properties

SessionInputOption.description: A choice in a select-style question.

id.description: Stable option identifier; for MCP enum values this is the enum string

recommended.description: Whether this option is the recommended/default choice

id.description: Stable question identifier used as the key in `answers`

required.description: Whether the user must answer this question to accept the request

SessionInputTextQuestion.description: Text question within a session input request.

id.description: Stable question identifier used as the key in `answers`

required.description: Whether the user must answer this question to accept the request

format.description: Format hint for text questions, such as `email`, `uri`, `date`, or `date-time`

SessionInputNumberQuestion.description: Numeric question within a session input request.

id.description: Stable question identifier used as the key in `answers`

required.description: Whether the user must answer this question to accept the request

SessionInputBooleanQuestion.description: Boolean question within a session input request.

id.description: Stable question identifier used as the key in `answers`

required.description: Whether the user must answer this question to accept the request

SessionInputSingleSelectQuestion.description: Single-select question within a session input request.

id.description: Stable question identifier used as the key in `answers`

required.description: Whether the user must answer this question to accept the request

options.description: Options the user may select from

allowFreeformInput.description: Whether the user may enter text instead of selecting an option

SessionInputMultiSelectQuestion.description: Multi-select question within a session input request.

id.description: Stable question identifier used as the key in `answers`

required.description: Whether the user must answer this question to accept the request

options.description: Options the user may select from

allowFreeformInput.description: Whether the user may enter text in addition to selecting options

SessionInputRequest.description:
A live request for user input.

The server creates or replaces requests with `session/inputRequested`.
Clients sync drafts with `session/inputAnswerChanged` and complete requests
with `session/inputCompleted`.

message.description: Display message for the request as a whole

url.description: URL the user should review or open, for URL-style elicitations

questions.description: Ordered questions to ask the user

answers.description: Current draft or submitted answers, keyed by question ID

freeformValues.description: Free-form text entered instead of selecting an option
Free-form text entered in addition to selected options
Free-form reason or value captured while skipping, if any

responseParts.description:
All response content in stream order: text, tool calls, reasoning, and content refs.

Consumers should derive display text by concatenating markdown parts,
and find tool calls by filtering for `ToolCall` parts.

ActiveTurn.description: An in-progress turn — the assistant is actively streaming.

responseParts.description:
All response content in stream order: text, tool calls, reasoning, and content refs.

Tool call parts include `pendingPermissions` when permissions are awaiting user approval.

UserMessage.description:
A user message and its associated attachments.

Attachments MAY be referenced inside {@link UserMessage.text} via their
{@link MessageAttachmentBase.rangeStart}/{@link MessageAttachmentBase.rangeEnd}
fields. Attachments without a range are still associated with the message
but do not correspond to a specific span in the text.

MessageAttachmentBase.description: Common fields shared by all {@link MessageAttachment} variants.

label.description:
A human-readable label for the attachment (e.g. the filename of a file
attachment). Used for display in UI.

rangeStart.description:
If defined, the start of the range in {@link UserMessage.text} that
references this attachment. The range is the half-open interval
`[rangeStart, rangeEnd)` of character offsets, measured in UTF-16 code
units.

When present, `rangeEnd` MUST also be present and MUST be greater than or
equal to `rangeStart`.

rangeEnd.description:
The end of the range in {@link UserMessage.text} that references this
attachment. See {@link rangeStart}.

displayKind.description:
Advisory display hint for clients rendering this attachment. Recognized
values include:

- `'image'`: the attachment is an image
- `'document'`: the attachment is a textual document
- `'symbol'`: the attachment is a code symbol (e.g. a function or class)
- `'directory'`: the attachment is a folder
- `'selection'`: the attachment is a selection within a document

Implementations MAY provide additional values; clients SHOULD fall back
to a reasonable default when an unknown value is encountered.

_meta.description:
Additional implementation-defined metadata for the attachment.

If the attachment was produced by the `completions` command, the client
MUST preserve every property of `_meta` originally returned by the agent
host when sending the user message containing the accepted completion.

SimpleMessageAttachment.description:
A simple, opaque attachment whose model representation is described by
the producer.

label.description:
A human-readable label for the attachment (e.g. the filename of a file
attachment). Used for display in UI.

rangeStart.description:
If defined, the start of the range in {@link UserMessage.text} that
references this attachment. The range is the half-open interval
`[rangeStart, rangeEnd)` of character offsets, measured in UTF-16 code
units.

When present, `rangeEnd` MUST also be present and MUST be greater than or
equal to `rangeStart`.

rangeEnd.description:
The end of the range in {@link UserMessage.text} that references this
attachment. See {@link rangeStart}.

displayKind.description:
Advisory display hint for clients rendering this attachment. Recognized
values include:

- `'image'`: the attachment is an image
- `'document'`: the attachment is a textual document
- `'symbol'`: the attachment is a code symbol (e.g. a function or class)
- `'directory'`: the attachment is a folder
- `'selection'`: the attachment is a selection within a document

Implementations MAY provide additional values; clients SHOULD fall back
to a reasonable default when an unknown value is encountered.

_meta.description:
Additional implementation-defined metadata for the attachment.

If the attachment was produced by the `completions` command, the client
MUST preserve every property of `_meta` originally returned by the agent
host when sending the user message containing the accepted completion.

modelRepresentation.description:
Representation of the attachment as it should be shown to the model.

If the attachment was produced by the client, this property MUST be
defined so the agent host can correctly interpret the attachment. This
property MAY be omitted when the attachment originated from a
`completions` response.

MessageEmbeddedResourceAttachment.description:
An attachment whose data is embedded inline as a base64 string.

Use this for small binary payloads (e.g. a pasted image) that should be
delivered with the user message itself rather than fetched separately.

label.description:
A human-readable label for the attachment (e.g. the filename of a file
attachment). Used for display in UI.

rangeStart.description:
If defined, the start of the range in {@link UserMessage.text} that
references this attachment. The range is the half-open interval
`[rangeStart, rangeEnd)` of character offsets, measured in UTF-16 code
units.

When present, `rangeEnd` MUST also be present and MUST be greater than or
equal to `rangeStart`.

rangeEnd.description:
The end of the range in {@link UserMessage.text} that references this
attachment. See {@link rangeStart}.

displayKind.description:
Advisory display hint for clients rendering this attachment. Recognized
values include:

- `'image'`: the attachment is an image
- `'document'`: the attachment is a textual document
- `'symbol'`: the attachment is a code symbol (e.g. a function or class)
- `'directory'`: the attachment is a folder
- `'selection'`: the attachment is a selection within a document

Implementations MAY provide additional values; clients SHOULD fall back
to a reasonable default when an unknown value is encountered.

_meta.description:
Additional implementation-defined metadata for the attachment.

If the attachment was produced by the `completions` command, the client
MUST preserve every property of `_meta` originally returned by the agent
host when sending the user message containing the accepted completion.

MessageResourceAttachment.description:
An attachment that references a resource by URI. The content is not
delivered inline; consumers can fetch it via `resourceRead` when needed.

label.description:
A human-readable label for the attachment (e.g. the filename of a file
attachment). Used for display in UI.

rangeStart.description:
If defined, the start of the range in {@link UserMessage.text} that
references this attachment. The range is the half-open interval
`[rangeStart, rangeEnd)` of character offsets, measured in UTF-16 code
units.

When present, `rangeEnd` MUST also be present and MUST be greater than or
equal to `rangeStart`.

rangeEnd.description:
The end of the range in {@link UserMessage.text} that references this
attachment. See {@link rangeStart}.

displayKind.description:
Advisory display hint for clients rendering this attachment. Recognized
values include:

- `'image'`: the attachment is an image
- `'document'`: the attachment is a textual document
- `'symbol'`: the attachment is a code symbol (e.g. a function or class)
- `'directory'`: the attachment is a folder
- `'selection'`: the attachment is a selection within a document

Implementations MAY provide additional values; clients SHOULD fall back
to a reasonable default when an unknown value is encountered.

_meta.description:
Additional implementation-defined metadata for the attachment.

If the attachment was produced by the `completions` command, the client
MUST preserve every property of `_meta` originally returned by the agent
host when sending the user message containing the accepted completion.

id.description: Part identifier, used by `session/delta` to target this part for content appends

ContentRef.description: A reference to large content stored outside the state tree.

ResourceReponsePart.description: A content part that's a reference to large content stored outside the state tree.

ToolCallResponsePart.description:
A tool call represented as a response part.

Tool calls are part of the response stream, interleaved with text and
reasoning. The `toolCall.toolCallId` serves as the part identifier for
actions that target this part.

id.description: Part identifier, used by `session/reasoning` to target this part for content appends

SystemNotificationResponsePart.description:
A system notification surfaced as part of the response stream.

System notifications are messages authored by the agent harness
that need to be visible to both the agent (for situational awareness) and
the user (for transcript continuity). Examples include "background subagent
X completed" or "task Y was cancelled".

content.description: The text of the system notification

ConfirmationOption.description:
A confirmation option that the server offers for a tool call awaiting
approval. Allows richer choices beyond simple approve/deny — for example,
"Approve in this Session" or "Deny with reason."

id.description: Unique identifier for the option, returned in the confirmed action

label.description: Human-readable label displayed to the user

kind.description: Whether this option represents an approval or denial

group.description:
Logical group number for visual categorisation.

Clients SHOULD display options in the order they are defined and MAY
use differing group numbers to insert dividers between logical clusters
of options.

ToolCallBase.description: Metadata common to all tool call states.

toolClientId.description:
If this tool is provided by a client, the `clientId` of the owning client.
Absent for server-side tools.

When set, the identified client is responsible for executing the tool and
dispatching `session/toolCallComplete` with the result.

_meta.description:
Additional provider-specific metadata for this tool call.

Clients MAY look for well-known keys here to provide enhanced UI.
For example, a `ptyTerminal` key with `{ input: string; output: string }`
indicates the tool operated on a terminal (both `input` and `output` may
contain escape sequences).

ToolCallParameterFields.description: Properties available once tool call parameters are fully received.

invocationMessage.description: Message describing what the tool will do

ToolCallResult.description: Tool execution result details, available after execution completes.

pastTenseMessage.description: Past-tense description of what the tool did

content.description:
Unstructured result content blocks.

This mirrors the `content` field of MCP `CallToolResult`.

structuredContent.description:
Optional structured result object.

This mirrors the `structuredContent` field of MCP `CallToolResult`.

error.description: Error details if the tool failed

ToolCallStreamingState.description: LM is streaming the tool call parameters.

toolClientId.description:
If this tool is provided by a client, the `clientId` of the owning client.
Absent for server-side tools.

When set, the identified client is responsible for executing the tool and
dispatching `session/toolCallComplete` with the result.

_meta.description:
Additional provider-specific metadata for this tool call.

Clients MAY look for well-known keys here to provide enhanced UI.
For example, a `ptyTerminal` key with `{ input: string; output: string }`
indicates the tool operated on a terminal (both `input` and `output` may
contain escape sequences).

invocationMessage.description: Progress message shown while parameters are streaming

ToolCallPendingConfirmationState.description:
Parameters are complete, or a running tool requires re-confirmation
(e.g. a mid-execution permission check).

toolClientId.description:
If this tool is provided by a client, the `clientId` of the owning client.
Absent for server-side tools.

When set, the identified client is responsible for executing the tool and
dispatching `session/toolCallComplete` with the result.

_meta.description:
Additional provider-specific metadata for this tool call.

Clients MAY look for well-known keys here to provide enhanced UI.
For example, a `ptyTerminal` key with `{ input: string; output: string }`
indicates the tool operated on a terminal (both `input` and `output` may
contain escape sequences).

invocationMessage.description: Message describing what the tool will do

confirmationTitle.description: Short title for the confirmation prompt (e.g. `"Run in terminal"`, `"Write file"`)

edits.description: File edits that this tool call will perform, for preview before confirmation

editable.description: Whether the agent host allows the client to edit the tool's input parameters before confirming

options.description:
Options the server offers for this confirmation. When present, the client
SHOULD render these instead of a plain approve/deny UI. Each option
belongs to a {@link ConfirmationOptionGroup} so the client can still
categorise the choices.

toolClientId.description:
If this tool is provided by a client, the `clientId` of the owning client.
Absent for server-side tools.

When set, the identified client is responsible for executing the tool and
dispatching `session/toolCallComplete` with the result.

_meta.description:
Additional provider-specific metadata for this tool call.

Clients MAY look for well-known keys here to provide enhanced UI.
For example, a `ptyTerminal` key with `{ input: string; output: string }`
indicates the tool operated on a terminal (both `input` and `output` may
contain escape sequences).

invocationMessage.description: Message describing what the tool will do

confirmed.description: How the tool was confirmed for execution

selectedOption.description: The confirmation option the user selected, if confirmation options were provided

content.description:
Partial content produced while the tool is still executing.

For example, a terminal content block lets clients subscribe to live
output before the tool completes.

ToolCallPendingResultConfirmationState.description: Tool finished executing, waiting for client to approve the result.

toolClientId.description:
If this tool is provided by a client, the `clientId` of the owning client.
Absent for server-side tools.

When set, the identified client is responsible for executing the tool and
dispatching `session/toolCallComplete` with the result.

_meta.description:
Additional provider-specific metadata for this tool call.

Clients MAY look for well-known keys here to provide enhanced UI.
For example, a `ptyTerminal` key with `{ input: string; output: string }`
indicates the tool operated on a terminal (both `input` and `output` may
contain escape sequences).

invocationMessage.description: Message describing what the tool will do

pastTenseMessage.description: Past-tense description of what the tool did

content.description:
Unstructured result content blocks.

This mirrors the `content` field of MCP `CallToolResult`.

structuredContent.description:
Optional structured result object.

This mirrors the `structuredContent` field of MCP `CallToolResult`.

error.description: Error details if the tool failed

confirmed.description: How the tool was confirmed for execution

selectedOption.description: The confirmation option the user selected, if confirmation options were provided

ToolCallCompletedState.description: Tool completed successfully or with an error.

error.description: Error details if the tool failed

confirmed.description: How the tool was confirmed for execution

selectedOption.description: The confirmation option the user selected, if confirmation options were provided

ToolCallCancelledState.description: Tool call was cancelled before execution.

toolClientId.description:
If this tool is provided by a client, the `clientId` of the owning client.
Absent for server-side tools.

When set, the identified client is responsible for executing the tool and

_meta.description:

Clients MAY look for well-known keys here to provide enhanced UI.
For example, a `ptyTerminal` key with `{ input: string; output: string }`

invocationMessage.description: Message describing what the tool will do

userSuggestion.description: What the user suggested doing instead

selectedOption.description: The confirmation option the user selected, if confirmation options were provided

ToolDefinition.description: Describes a tool available in a session, provided by either the server or the active client.

description.description: Description of what the tool does

inputSchema.description:
JSON Schema defining the expected input parameters.

Optional because client-provided tools may not have formal schemas.
Mirrors MCP `Tool.inputSchema`.

outputSchema.description:
JSON Schema defining the structure of the tool's output.

Mirrors MCP `Tool.outputSchema`.

annotations.description: Behavioral hints about the tool. All properties are advisory.

_meta.description:
Additional provider-specific metadata.

Mirrors the MCP `_meta` convention.

ToolAnnotations.description:
Behavioral hints about a tool. All properties are advisory and not
guaranteed to faithfully describe tool behavior.

Mirrors MCP `ToolAnnotations` from the Model Context Protocol specification.

readOnlyHint.description: Tool does not modify its environment (default: false)

destructiveHint.description: Tool may perform destructive updates (default: true)

idempotentHint.description: Repeated calls with the same arguments have no additional effect (default: false)

openWorldHint.description: Tool may interact with external entities (default: true)

ToolResultTextContent.description:
Text content in a tool result.

Mirrors MCP `TextContent`.

ToolResultEmbeddedResourceContent.description:
Base64-encoded binary content embedded in a tool result.

Mirrors MCP `EmbeddedResource` for inline binary data.

ToolResultResourceContent.description:
A reference to a resource stored outside the tool result.

Wraps {@link ContentRef} for lazy-loading large results.

FileEdit.description:
Describes a file modification with before/after state and diff metadata.

Supports creates (only `after`), deletes (only `before`), renames/moves
(different `uri` in `before` and `after`), and edits (same `uri`, different content).

before.description: The file state before the edit. Absent for file creations or for in-place file edits.

after.description: The file state after the edit. Absent for file deletions.

ToolResultFileEditContent.description: Describes a file modification performed by a tool.

before.description: The file state before the edit. Absent for file creations or for in-place file edits.

after.description: The file state after the edit. Absent for file deletions.

ToolResultTerminalContent.description:
A reference to a terminal whose output is relevant to this tool result.

Clients can subscribe to the terminal's URI to stream its output in real
time, providing live feedback while a tool is executing.

resource.description: Terminal URI (subscribable for full terminal state)

title.description: Display title for the terminal content

ToolResultSubagentContent.description:
A reference to a subagent session spawned by a tool.

Clients can subscribe to the subagent's session URI to stream its
progress in real time, including inner tool calls and responses.

resource.description: Subagent session URI (subscribable for full session state)

description.description: Human-readable description of the subagent's task

CustomizationRef.description:
A reference to an [Open Plugins](https://open-plugins.com/) plugin.

This is intentionally thin — AHP specifies plugin identity and metadata
but not implementation details, which are defined by the Open Plugins spec.

uri.description: Plugin URI (e.g. an HTTPS URL or marketplace identifier)

description.description: Description of what the plugin provides

nonce.description:
Opaque version token for this customization.

Clients SHOULD include a nonce with every customization they provide.
Consumers can compare nonces to detect whether a customization has
changed since it was last seen, avoiding redundant reloads or copies.

SessionCustomization.description: A customization active in a session.

customization.description: The plugin this customization refers to

enabled.description: Whether this customization is currently enabled

clientId.description:
The `clientId` of the client that contributed this customization.
Absent for server-provided customizations.

statusMessage.description: Human-readable status detail (e.g. error message or degradation warning).

TerminalInfo.description: Lightweight terminal metadata exposed on the root state.

resource.description: Terminal URI (subscribable for full terminal state)

exitCode.description: Process exit code, if the terminal process has exited

TerminalClientClaim.description: A terminal claimed by a connected client.

TerminalSessionClaim.description: A terminal claimed by a session, optionally scoped to a specific turn or tool call.

session.description: Session URI that claimed the terminal

turnId.description: Optional turn identifier within the session

toolCallId.description: Optional tool call identifier within the turn

TerminalState.description: Full state for a single terminal, loaded when a client subscribes to the terminal's URI.

cwd.description: Current working directory of the terminal process

content.description:
Typed content parts, replacing the flat `content: string`.

Naive consumers that only need the raw VT stream can reconstruct it with:
  `content.map(p => p.type === 'command' ? p.output : p.value).join('')`

Consumers that need command boundaries can filter by part type.

exitCode.description: Process exit code, set when the terminal process exits

supportsCommandDetection.description:
Whether this terminal emits `terminal/commandExecuted` and
`terminal/commandFinished` actions and populates `command`-typed parts.

Clients MUST check this flag before relying on command detection.
Do NOT use the presence of a `command` part as a feature flag — parts
are absent in the normal idle state.

TerminalUnclassifiedPart.description:
Unstructured terminal output — content before, between, or after commands,
or from terminals that do not support command detection.

value.description: Accumulated VT output. Appended to by `terminal/data` when no command is executing.

TerminalCommandPart.description:
A single command: its command line and the output it produced.

While `isComplete` is false the command is still executing; `output` grows
as `terminal/data` actions arrive. At `terminal/commandFinished` the part
is mutated in-place with `isComplete: true` and the completion metadata.

commandId.description:
Stable id matching the `commandId` on the corresponding
`terminal/commandExecuted` and `terminal/commandFinished` actions.

commandLine.description: The command line submitted to the shell.

output.description:
Accumulated VT output. Appended to by `terminal/data` while `isComplete`
is false. Shell integration escape sequences are stripped by the server.

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp (ms) when execution started, as reported by the server.

exitCode.description: Shell exit code. Set at completion. `undefined` if unknown.

durationMs.description: Wall-clock duration in milliseconds. Set at completion.

Snapshot.description:
A point-in-time snapshot of a subscribed resource's state, returned by
`initialize`, `reconnect`, and `subscribe`.

state.description: The current state of the resource

fromSeq.description: The `serverSeq` at which this snapshot was taken. Subsequent actions will have `serverSeq > fromSeq`.

InitializeParams.description:
Establishes a new connection and negotiates the protocol version.
This MUST be the first message sent by the client.

protocolVersions.description:
Protocol versions the client is willing to speak, ordered from most
preferred to least preferred. Each entry is a [SemVer](https://semver.org)
`MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH` string (e.g. `"0.1.0"`).

The server selects one entry and returns it as `InitializeResult.protocolVersion`.
If the server cannot speak any of the offered versions, it MUST return
error code `-32005` (`UnsupportedProtocolVersion`).

initialSubscriptions.description: URIs to subscribe to during handshake

locale.description:
IETF BCP 47 language tag indicating the client's preferred locale
(e.g. `"en-US"`, `"ja"`). The server SHOULD use this to localise
user-facing strings such as confirmation option labels.

InitializeResult.description:
Result of the `initialize` command.

`protocolVersion` is the version the server has selected from the client's
`protocolVersions` list. The client and server MUST use this version for
the rest of the connection. If the server cannot speak any of the offered
versions it MUST return error code `-32005` (`UnsupportedProtocolVersion`)
instead of a result.

protocolVersion.description:
Protocol version selected by the server. MUST be one of the entries in
`InitializeParams.protocolVersions`. Formatted as a [SemVer](https://semver.org)
`MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH` string (e.g. `"0.1.0"`).

defaultDirectory.description: Suggested default directory for remote filesystem browsing

completionTriggerCharacters.description:
Characters that, when typed in a {@link UserMessage} input, SHOULD cause
the client to issue a `completions` request with
{@link CompletionItemKind.UserMessage}. Typically includes characters like
`'@'` or `'/'`.

ReconnectParams.description:
Re-establishes a dropped connection. The server replays missed actions or
provides fresh snapshots.

clientId.description: Client identifier from the original connection

subscriptions.description: URIs the client was subscribed to

ReconnectReplayResult.description:
Reconnect result when the server can replay from the requested sequence.

The server MUST include all replayed data in the response.

missing.description:
URIs from `ReconnectParams.subscriptions` that the server cannot resume.
This includes resources that no longer exist (e.g. disposed sessions or
terminals) as well as resources the client is no longer permitted to
observe. Clients SHOULD drop these from their local subscription set.

ReconnectSnapshotResult.description: Reconnect result when the gap exceeds the replay buffer.

SubscribeParams.description: Subscribe to a URI-identified state resource.

SessionForkSource.description:
Creates a new session with the specified agent provider.

If the session URI already exists, the server MUST return an error with code
`-32003` (`SessionAlreadyExists`).

After creation, the client should subscribe to the session URI to receive state
updates. The server also broadcasts a `notify/sessionAdded` notification to all
clients.

session.description: URI of the existing session to fork from

turnId.description: Turn ID in the source session; content up to and including this turn's response is copied

model.description: Model selection (ID and optional model-specific configuration)

agent.description: Custom agent selection (name) — selects a customization-pipeline agent for this session

fork.description:
Fork from an existing session. The new session is populated with content
from the source session up to and including the specified turn's response.

config.description:
Agent-specific configuration values collected via `resolveSessionConfig`.
Keys and values correspond to the schema returned by the server.

activeClient.description:
Eagerly claim the active client role for the new session.

When provided, the server initializes the session with this client as the
active client, equivalent to dispatching a `session/activeClientChanged`
action immediately after creation. The `clientId` MUST match the
`clientId` the creating client supplied in `initialize`.

DisposeSessionParams.description:
Disposes a session and cleans up server-side resources.

The server broadcasts a `notify/sessionRemoved` notification to all clients.

CreateTerminalParams.description:
Creates a new terminal on the server.

After creation, the client should subscribe to the terminal URI to receive
state updates. The server dispatches `root/terminalsChanged` to update the
root terminal list.

DisposeTerminalParams.description:
Disposes a terminal and kills its process if still running.

The server dispatches `root/terminalsChanged` to remove the terminal from
the root terminal list.

ListSessionsParams.description:
Returns a list of session summaries. Used to populate session lists and sidebars.

The session list is **not** part of the state tree because it can be arbitrarily
large. Clients fetch it imperatively and maintain a local cache updated by
`notify/sessionAdded` and `notify/sessionRemoved` notifications.

ResourceReadParams.description:
Reads the content of a resource by URI.

Content references keep the state tree small by storing large data (images,
long tool outputs) by reference rather than inline.

Binary content (images, etc.) MUST use `base64` encoding. Text content MAY
use `utf-8` encoding.

encoding.description: Preferred encoding for the returned data (default: server-chosen)

ResourceReadResult.description:
Result of the `resourceRead` command.

The server SHOULD honor the `encoding` requested in the params. If the
server cannot provide the requested encoding, it MUST fall back to either
`base64` or `utf-8`.

ResourceWriteParams.description:
Writes content to a file on the server's filesystem.

Binary content (images, etc.) MUST use `base64` encoding. Text content MAY
use `utf-8` encoding.

If the file does not exist, it is created. If the file already exists, it is
overwritten unless `createOnly` is set.

uri.description: Target file URI on the server filesystem

createOnly.description:
If `true`, the server MUST fail if the file already exists instead of
overwriting it. Useful for safe creation of new files.

ResourceWriteResult.description:
Result of the `resourceWrite` command.

An empty object on success.

ResourceListParams.description:
Lists directory entries at a file URI on the server's filesystem.

This is intended for remote folder pickers and similar UI that needs to let
users navigate the server's local filesystem.

The server MUST return success only if the target exists and is a directory.
If the target does not exist, is not a directory, or cannot be accessed, the
server MUST return a JSON-RPC error.

uri.description: Directory URI on the server filesystem

type.description: Whether the entry is a file or directory

entries.description: Entries directly contained in the requested directory

FetchTurnsParams.description:
Fetches historical turns for a session. Used for lazy loading of conversation
history.

before.description: Turn ID to fetch before (exclusive). Omit to fetch from the most recent turn.

limit.description: Maximum number of turns to return. Server MAY impose its own upper bound.

hasMore.description: Whether more turns exist before the returned range

UnsubscribeParams.description: Stop receiving updates for a URI.

DispatchActionParams.description:
Fire-and-forget action dispatch (write-ahead). The client applies actions
optimistically to local state.

ResourceCopyParams.description:
Copies a resource from one URI to another on the server's filesystem.

If the destination already exists, it is overwritten unless `failIfExists`
is set.

failIfExists.description:
If `true`, the server MUST fail if the destination already exists instead
of overwriting it.

ResourceCopyResult.description:
Result of the `resourceCopy` command.

An empty object on success.

ResourceDeleteParams.description: Deletes a resource at a URI on the server's filesystem.

uri.description: URI of the resource to delete

recursive.description:
If `true` and the target is a directory, delete it and all its contents
recursively. If `false` (default), deleting a non-empty directory MUST fail.

ResourceDeleteResult.description:
Result of the `resourceDelete` command.

An empty object on success.

ResourceRequestParams.description:
Requests permission to access a resource on the receiver's filesystem.

`resourceRequest` is symmetrical and MAY be sent in either direction: a
client asks the server to grant access to a server-side resource, or a
server asks the client to grant access to a client-side resource. The
receiver decides whether to allow, deny, or prompt the user for the
requested access.

If the receiver denies access, it MUST respond with `PermissionDenied`
(-32009). The error data MAY include a `ResourceRequestParams` value
describing the access the caller would need to be granted for the
operation to succeed; see `PermissionDeniedErrorData` in
`types/errors.ts`.

After a successful `resourceRequest`, the caller MAY use the corresponding
`resource*` commands (e.g. `resourceRead`, `resourceWrite`) to perform the
operation. Receivers MAY rescind access at any time by returning
`PermissionDenied` on subsequent operations.

Either `read`, `write`, or both SHOULD be set to `true`. A request with
neither flag set is treated as `read: true` by receivers.

uri.description:
Resource URI being requested. Typically a `file:` URI on the receiver's
filesystem, but any URI scheme that the receiver mediates access to is
allowed.

read.description: Whether the caller needs read access to the resource.

write.description: Whether the caller needs write access to the resource.

ResourceRequestResult.description:
Result of the `resourceRequest` command.

An empty object on success.

ResourceMoveParams.description:
Moves (renames) a resource from one URI to another on the server's filesystem.

If the destination already exists, it is overwritten unless `failIfExists`
is set.

failIfExists.description:
If `true`, the server MUST fail if the destination already exists instead
of overwriting it.

ResourceMoveResult.description:
Result of the `resourceMove` command.

An empty object on success.

AuthenticateParams.description:
Pushes a Bearer token for a protected resource. The `resource` field MUST
match a `ProtectedResourceMetadata.resource` value declared by an agent
in `AgentInfo.protectedResources`.

Tokens are delivered using [RFC 6750](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6750)
(Bearer Token Usage) semantics. The client obtains the token from the
authorization server(s) listed in the resource's metadata and pushes it
to the server via this command.

resource.description:
The protected resource identifier. MUST match a `resource` value from
`ProtectedResourceMetadata` declared in `AgentInfo.protectedResources`.

token.description: Bearer token obtained from the resource's authorization server

AuthenticateResult.description:
Result of the `authenticate` command.

An empty object on success. If the token is invalid or the resource is
unrecognized, the server MUST return a JSON-RPC error (e.g. `AuthRequired`
`-32007` or `InvalidParams` `-32602`).

ResolveSessionConfigParams.description:
Iteratively resolves the session configuration schema. The client sends the
current partial session config and any user-filled metadata values. The server
returns a property schema describing what additional metadata is needed,
contextual to the current selections.

The client calls this command whenever the user changes a significant input
(e.g. picks a working directory, toggles a property). Each response returns
the full current property set (not a delta). The returned `values` contain
server-resolved defaults to pass to `createSession`.

schema.description: JSON Schema describing available configuration properties given the current context

values.description: Current configuration values (echoed back with server-resolved defaults applied)

SessionConfigValueItem.description: A single value item returned by `sessionConfigCompletions`.

value.description: The value to store in config

SessionConfigCompletionsParams.description:
Queries the server for allowed values of a dynamic session config property.

Used when a property in the schema returned by `resolveSessionConfig` has
`enumDynamic: true`. The client sends a search query and receives matching
values with display metadata.

config.description: Current user-filled configuration values (provides context for the query)

property.description: Property id from the schema to query values for

query.description: Search filter text (empty or omitted returns default/recent values)

CompletionsParams.description:
Requests completion items for a partially-typed input (e.g. a user message
the user is currently composing). Used to power `@`-mention pickers,
file/symbol references, and similar inline-completion experiences.

Servers SHOULD treat this command as best-effort and return promptly. The
client SHOULD debounce calls to avoid flooding the server with requests on
every keystroke.

kind.description: What kind of completion is being requested.

session.description: The session URI the completion is being requested for.

text.description:
The complete text of the input being completed (e.g. the full user
message text typed so far).

offset.description:
The character offset within `text` at which the completion is requested,
measured in UTF-16 code units. MUST satisfy `0 <= offset <= text.length`.

CompletionItem.description:
A single completion item returned by the `completions` command.

When the user accepts an item, the client SHOULD:
1. Replace the range `[rangeStart, rangeEnd)` in the input with `insertText`
   (or insert `insertText` at the cursor when the range is omitted).
2. Associate the item's `attachment` with the resulting {@link UserMessage}.

insertText.description: The text inserted into the input when this item is accepted.

rangeStart.description:
If defined, the start of the range in the input's `text` that is replaced
by `insertText`. The range is the half-open interval
`[rangeStart, rangeEnd)` of character offsets, measured in UTF-16 code
units.

When omitted, the client SHOULD insert `insertText` at the cursor.

Note: this range refers to positions in the *current* input. The
attachment's own `rangeStart`/`rangeEnd` (when present) refer to
positions in the final {@link UserMessage.text} after the item is
accepted.

rangeEnd.description:
The end of the range in the input's `text` that is replaced by
`insertText`. See {@link rangeStart}.

attachment.description: The attachment associated with this completion item.

items.description: The completion items, in the order the server suggests displaying them.
